name: Orbital Proliferation
description: Launch costs fall, ground grids stay carbon-intensive. Fast orbital expansion; bandwidth (not energy) becomes the binding constraint; ground station ownership becomes the new chokepoint.
dominant_use_case: General cloud compute migration to orbit

cla_framing.litany: Orbital compute is cheaper and cleaner than ground-based alternatives
cla_framing.system: Launch cost reduction enables orbital infrastructure build-out faster than grid decarbonization
cla_framing.worldview: Technological progress solves environmental problems through displacement rather than reform
cla_framing.metaphor: The frontier — new territory absorbs the externalities the old territory couldn't handle

name: The Grid Transition Trap
description: Launch costs fall, but ground grids decarbonize fast. Orbital's environmental case weakens in clean-grid regions; niches into satellite-data processing rather than general cloud.

cla_framing.litany: Ground data centers are already going green — orbital compute solves a problem that's disappearing
cla_framing.system: Grid decarbonization outpaces orbital build-out, removing the carbon arbitrage
cla_framing.worldview: Incremental reform of existing systems beats revolutionary displacement
cla_framing.metaphor: The race — two solutions running toward the same finish line, one already closer

name: Compute Sovereignty Wars
description: Geopolitical fragmentation produces parallel constellations with no cross-constellation data flow. Spectrum and ground-station rights become diplomatic terrain.

cla_framing.litany: Nations are building their own orbital compute to avoid dependency on foreign infrastructure
cla_framing.system: Data sovereignty regulation fragments the orbital commons into national enclaves
cla_framing.worldview: Infrastructure is power — who controls the compute layer controls the information layer
cla_framing.metaphor: The enclosure — the orbital commons fenced into sovereign parcels

name: The Bandwidth Ceiling
description: Orbital compute scales faster than ground-station laser infrastructure. Satellite-data-processing model holds; general cloud via orbital stalls.

cla_framing.litany: We can put compute in space but can't get enough data up and down
cla_framing.system: Ground station infrastructure lags orbital deployment, creating a bottleneck at the Earth-space boundary
cla_framing.worldview: Every infrastructure revolution relocates its bottleneck rather than eliminating it
cla_framing.metaphor: The hourglass — the narrow neck between two expanding chambers

name: The Debris Break
description: A high-profile LEO collision triggers an insurance/regulatory freeze on orbital DC expansion. GEO and lunar assets above the debris band become relatively more attractive.

cla_framing.litany: A collision event halted orbital data center expansion
cla_framing.system: Kessler-adjacent risk reprices LEO insurance and triggers regulatory freeze
cla_framing.worldview: Commons tragedies recur — the orbital commons is no exception to overexploitation
cla_framing.metaphor: The shatter — one break cascades through the shared space

name: The In-Orbit Economy
description: Onboard processing of satellite-generated data becomes mainstream; raw data rarely touches the ground. The least speculative scenario.

cla_framing.litany: Satellites process their own data now — only insights come down
cla_framing.system: Edge computing logic applied at orbital scale eliminates the downlink bottleneck for sensor data
cla_framing.worldview: Processing should happen where data is generated, not where infrastructure was historically built
cla_framing.metaphor: The refinery — raw ore processed at the mine, only refined product shipped
